theorem Filter.prod_le_map {α β γ : Type*} [Nonempty γ] {f : β → α}
  {ℓ : Filter β} {m : Filter γ} :
    m ≤ Filter.map f ℓ ↔ ∀ (s : Set γ), m.eventually (· ∈ s) → ℓ.eventually (· ∈ f ⁻¹' s) := by
  constructor <;> intro h s hs
  · -- Prove the forward direction: m ≤ Filter.map f ℓ → ∀ (s : Set γ), m.eventually (· ∈ s) → ℓ.eventually (· ∈ f ⁻¹' s)
    have h₁ : m ≤ Filter.map f ℓ := h
    have h₂ : m.eventually (· ∈ s) := hs
    have h₃ : (Filter.map f ℓ).eventually (· ∈ s) := h₁ h₂
    -- Since f ⁻¹' s is in ℓ and f maps it to s, we can conclude the proof
    have h₄ : ℓ.eventually (· ∈ f ⁻¹' s) := by
      have h₅ : (Filter.map f ℓ).eventually (· ∈ s) := h₃
      -- Use the fact that f ⁻¹' s is in ℓ to get the desired result
      have h₆ : ℓ.eventually (· ∈ f ⁻¹' s) := by
        -- Use the property of the image filter to get the desired result
        have h₇ : (Filter.map f ℓ).eventually (· ∈ s) := h₅
        have h₈ : ℓ.eventually (· ∈ f ⁻¹' s) := by
          -- Use the fact that f ⁻¹' s is in ℓ to get the desired result
          have h₉ : (Filter.map f ℓ).eventually (· ∈ s) := h₇
          have h₁₀ : ℓ.eventually (· ∈ f ⁻¹' s) := by
            -- Use the property of the image filter to get the desired result
            have h₁₁ : (Filter.map f ℓ).eventually (· ∈ s) := h₉
            have h₁₂ : ℓ.eventually (· ∈ f ⁻¹' s) := by
              -- Use the fact that f ⁻¹' s is in ℓ to get the desired result
              simpa [Filter.eventually_map] using h₁₁
            exact h₁₂
          exact h₁₀
        exact h₈
      exact h₆
    exact h₄
  · -- Prove the reverse direction: (∀ (s : Set γ), m.eventually (· ∈ s) → ℓ.eventually (· ∈ f ⁻¹' s)) → m ≤ Filter.map f ℓ
    intro h
    -- Use the fact that m ≤ Filter.map f ℓ if and only if for all s, m.eventually (· ∈ s) → ℓ.eventually (· ∈ f ⁻¹' s)
    have h₁ : m ≤ Filter.map f ℓ := by
      -- Use the property of the image filter to get the desired result
      refine' Filter.le_map_iff_le_comap.mpr _
      -- Use the fact that m ≤ Filter.map f ℓ if and only if for all s, m.eventually (· ∈ s) → ℓ.eventually (· ∈ f ⁻¹' s)
      intro s hs
      -- Use the fact that m ≤ Filter.map f ℓ if and only if for all s, m.eventually (· ∈ s) → ℓ.eventually (· ∈ f ⁻¹' s)
      have h₂ : m.eventually (· ∈ s) := hs
      have h₃ : ℓ.eventually (· ∈ f ⁻¹' s) := h s h₂
      -- Use the fact that f ⁻¹' s is in ℓ to get the desired result
      have h₄ : ℓ.eventually (· ∈ f ⁻¹' s) := h₃
      -- Use the property of the image filter to get the desired result
      simpa [Filter.eventually_comap, Filter.mem_preimage] using h₄
    -- Use the fact that m ≤ Filter.map f ℓ if and only if for all s, m.eventually (· ∈ s) → ℓ.eventually (· ∈ f ⁻¹' s)
    exact h₁

  {ℓ : Filter β} {m : Filter γ} :
    m ≤ Filter.map f ℓ ↔ ∀ (s : Set γ), m.eventually (· ∈ s) → ℓ.eventually (· ∈ f ⁻¹' s) := by
  constructor <;> intro h s hs
  · -- Prove the forward direction: m ≤ Filter.map f ℓ → ∀ (s : Set γ), m.eventually (· ∈ s) → ℓ.eventually (· ∈ f ⁻¹' s)
    have h₁ : m ≤ Filter.map f ℓ := h
    have h₂ : m.eventually (· ∈ s) := hs
    have h₃ : (Filter.map f ℓ).eventually (· ∈ s) := h₁ h₂
    -- Since f ⁻¹' s is in ℓ and f maps it to s, we can conclude the proof

      have h₅ : (Filter.map f ℓ).eventually (· ∈ s) := h₃
      -- Use the fact that f ⁻¹' s is in ℓ to get the desired result

-- Prove that the predictable sigma-algebra is less than or equal to the product sigma-algebra
  have h₁ : 𝓝.predictable ≤ mΩ ⊗ₘ measurableSpace T := by
    -- Use the fact that the predictable sigma-algebra is generated by predictable sets
    -- and that the product sigma-algebra contains all measurable rectangles
    refine' le_trans _ (le_of_eq (by rw [Prod.instMeasurableSpace]))
    -- The predictable sigma-algebra is less than or equal to the product of mΩ and the Borel sigma-algebra on T
    -- because all predictable sets are measurable with respect to the product sigma-algebra
    apply σAlgebra.ext_le
    · intro s hs
      -- Each set in the predictable sigma-algebra is in the product sigma-algebra
      have h₂ : s ∈ 𝓝.predictable := hs
      have h₃ : s ∈ mΩ ⊗ₘ measurableSpace T := by
        -- Use the fact that the product sigma-algebra contains all measurable rectangles
        -- and that the predictable sets are generated by such rectangles
        exact (Inf_le _ |>.trans (le_refl _)).symm h₂
      exact h₃
    · intro s hs
      -- Each set in the product sigma-algebra is in the predictable sigma-algebra
      have h₂ : s ∈ mΩ ⊗ₘ measurableSpace T := hs
      have h₃ : s ∈ 𝓝.predictable := by
        -- Use the fact that the product sigma-algebra is contained in the predictable sigma-algebra
        -- because all measurable rectangles are predictable
        exact le_Inf (fun t => le_of_eq (by simp_all [Filtration.predictable])) h₂
      exact h₃
  -- Since the product sigma-algebra is equal to the product of mΩ and the Borel sigma-algebra on T,
  -- we can conclude the proof
  exact h₁
